status.summary: Obsidian runtime lookup and insertion flow is validated after parser, requestUrl transport, Gesetze im Internet extraction, local LawSectionCache implementation, minimal cache settings, lookup-modal metadata footer toggle, StGB support, and canonical StGB display normalization. Tests and production build pass; runtime validation confirmed StGB § 242 displays as StGB, not STGB.

blockers.title: Primary law data source not yet validated
blockers.notes: Initial target is Gesetze im Internet / official federal law texts. Need technical validation of URL/XML/HTML access pattern before implementing fetcher.

blockers.title: NeuRIS API stability and coverage must be verified
blockers.notes: NeuRIS/Rechtsinformationsportal is the strategic target provider, but current service is in test phase and data coverage is limited.

issues.title: Citation and retrieval metadata required
issues.notes: Every inserted law text must include source, law code, section and retrieval date.

issues.title: Scope must remain Bundesrecht-only for MVP
issues.notes: No Landesrecht, EU law, case law or commentary in MVP.

tasks.title: Create Obsidian plugin scaffold
Implement legal citation parser
Implement mock law source
Insert formatted law text into active note
Implement provider registry
Implement mock API provider before real network providers
Validate NeuRIS API endpoints and schema
Validate Gesetze im Internet XML fallback access
Implement GesetzeImInternetProvider fixture fallback for BGB § 823
tasks.notes: Added fixture-tested HTML fallback for BGB § 823 with official-source/non-authoritative metadata.

tasks.title: Update provider order to NeuRIS, Gesetze im Internet, Mock
Handle Gesetze im Internet 404 as unresolved null
Handle non-404 Gesetze im Internet failures as LawProviderUnavailableError
Normalize Gesetze im Internet letter suffix URLs to lowercase
Live-validate full provider chain for BGB § 823
tasks.notes: Reference: BGB § 823. Provider call order: neuris -> gesetze-im-internet. Final providerId: gesetze-im-internet. MockLawProvider called: false. LawProviderUnavailableError thrown: false. sourceUrl: https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/bgb/__823.html. Result: resolved via Gesetze im Internet fallback.

tasks.title: Expand Gesetze im Internet law-code support beyond BGB
Implement cache layer for retrieved law sections
tasks.notes: Local LawSectionCache implemented. Cache key format is LAWCODE:section. lawCode is uppercased for the key; section is trimmed and lowercased for the key only, so letter-suffixed sections such as 312G and 312g resolve to the same cache key. Live provider chain remains authoritative and is called first. Successful live LawSection results are cached best-effort; cache write failures do not fail the lookup. Cache fallback is only checked after live providers return null. Provider failures remain terminal and must not be hidden by cache. null/not-found results are not cached. Cached mock-provider entries are ignored unless MockLawProvider is explicitly enabled. Tests cover cache key normalization, live-first behavior, provider failure behavior, best-effort cache writes, and mock-cache isolation.

tasks.title: Disable MockLawProvider outside development/test mode
tasks.notes: Default provider order is now: 1. neuris, 2. gesetze-im-internet, 3. no mock. MockLawProvider can only be enabled explicitly with enableMockLawProvider: true. ProviderRegistry behavior remains unchanged: null falls through and LawProviderUnavailableError remains terminal. Plugin startup still performs no law-provider network calls.

tasks.title: Implement note insertion UX for formatted law text
tasks.notes: After successful lookup, the modal shows 'In aktuelle Note einfügen'. Insertion uses CitationFormatter markdown and happens only after explicit user click. The formatted law section is inserted at the current cursor position in the active Markdown editor. If no active Markdown editor exists, the plugin shows an Obsidian Notice. Stale lookup protection remains preserved.

tasks.title: Fix Obsidian runtime transport for external law-provider HTTP
tasks.notes: Global fetch worked in Node validation but failed in Obsidian runtime for Gesetze im Internet. Runtime providers now use Obsidian requestUrl transport. Constructor injection remains available for fixture tests, and startup still performs no law-provider network calls.

tasks.title: Clean Gesetze im Internet section extraction for BGB § 823
tasks.notes: Obsidian runtime validation confirmed BGB § 823 resolves through Gesetze im Internet. Extraction now prefers norm paragraph blocks and excludes footer/navigation artifacts. Confirmed removed artifacts: zum Seitenanfang, Impressum, Datenschutz, Barrierefreiheitserklärung, Feedback-Formular, Seite ausdrucken. Legal paragraphs (1) and (2) remain present. The insert button appears after successful lookup.

tasks.title: Optionally render lookup result preview instead of raw Markdown display
tasks.notes: Current lookup result can display raw Markdown. A future UX pass may render the preview while preserving formatted Markdown insertion.

tasks.title: Manually validate insertion into an active Obsidian note
tasks.notes: Obsidian runtime plugin test succeeded. Lookup flow, visible insertion action, and insertion into an active note were manually validated after the cache layer and parser improvements.

tasks.title: Improve law-reference input placeholder and section-first parser coverage
tasks.notes: Placeholder changed to "z. B. § 823 BGB". Parser now accepts section-first input without §, including "823 BGB", "823a BGB", "312g BGB", and "312G BGB". Existing supported forms remain supported: "§ 823 BGB", "BGB 823", "BGB § 823", "KAGB § 1", and "GwG 10". No provider, registry, or transport behavior changed.

tasks.title: Add minimal local cache settings
tasks.notes: Added settings for enableLawSectionCache, default true, and lawSectionCacheTtlDays, default null. The settings UI can disable the cache wrapper or set a positive TTL in days. Cache settings preserve live-first provider semantics: live providers are still called first, cache fallback is only checked after live providers return null, provider failures remain terminal, null/not-found results are not cached, and cached mock-provider entries remain gated by explicit MockLawProvider opt-in.

tasks.title: Add lookup-modal toggle for inserted source/cache metadata footer
tasks.notes: Runtime validation succeeded. The lookup modal toggle "Quellen- und Cache-Hinweis einfügen" is visible, persists its selected state, and controls whether inserted Markdown includes the Quelle/Cache footer. With the toggle enabled, the existing metadata footer is inserted. With the toggle disabled, the inserted law text keeps the heading and quoted section text but omits the footer. Lookup and insertion behavior remain functional.

tasks.title: Record additional runtime law-code coverage observations
tasks.notes: Manual Obsidian runtime testing after the metadata footer toggle confirmed additional successful law-code lookups for KAGB and STAG. StGB lookup did not work and should be investigated separately before expanding Gesetze im Internet coverage.

tasks.title: Investigate failed StGB lookup
tasks.notes: Root cause: StGB already parsed correctly through the generic uppercasing parser path, but GesetzeImInternetProvider did not support STGB in supportedLaws, so no Gesetze im Internet URL was generated and the provider returned null without fetching. Added parser coverage for StGB input variants, added STGB -> stgb / Strafgesetzbuch mapping, and added provider tests for https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/stgb/__242.html while preserving provider fallback, 404/null, non-404/LawProviderUnavailableError, transport, cache, and UI invariants. Subsequent Obsidian runtime validation confirmed that StGB lookup, insertion, and the metadata toggle now work end to end.

tasks.title: Normalize canonical display abbreviation for StGB output
tasks.notes: Root cause: internal lookup code remains uppercase by parser and lookup design, but the mapped LawSection reused STGB for display, so inserted output showed '§ 242 STGB' instead of the canonical legal abbreviation '§ 242 StGB'. Initial live-result mapping fix corrected fresh GesetzeImInternetProvider results, but review identified a P2 upgrade-path gap: pre-existing cached STGB LawSection entries could still surface uppercase STGB during cache fallback because CitationFormatter renders section.lawCode verbatim. The fix adds a small shared canonical display-law-code helper, keeps parser normalization, provider lookup keys, cache keys, requestUrl transport, provider fallback semantics, and metadata-footer toggle behavior unchanged, and normalizes cached pre-existing STGB entries on fallback/read before returning the cached LawSection. Runtime validation confirmed that StGB § 242 now displays and inserts as "§ 242 StGB – Diebstahl" rather than "§ 242 STGB – Diebstahl".

mvp_flow: Plugin inserts text into active note
invariants: Plugin must not modify notes without explicit user action
Inserted law text may omit the source/cache footer only when the user explicitly disables it in the lookup modal
MVP must only handle German federal law
Fetching logic must remain separate from UI logic

decisions.title: Use Codex for repository edits and ChatGPT for architecture/debugging
Use API-first provider architecture
decisions.notes: The plugin should retrieve German law texts via APIs where possible. Provider abstraction is required to support NeuRIS, Gesetze im Internet fallback, and optional Open Legal Data.

decisions.title: Use Gesetze im Internet as fallback for confirmed NeuRIS coverage gaps
decisions.notes: Gesetze im Internet is used as the official/public consolidated-law fallback for confirmed NeuRIS coverage gaps, starting with BGB § 823.

decisions.title: Provider fallback lifecycle live-validated for BGB § 823
decisions.notes: NeuRIS coverage gaps may fall through to Gesetze im Internet when the first provider returns null, but provider failures must remain terminal.

decisions.title: MockLawProvider requires explicit development/demo opt-in
decisions.notes: MockLawProvider must not be registered in normal runtime. It is development/demo only and requires explicit opt-in.

decisions.title: Note insertion requires explicit user action
decisions.notes: Law text insertion must require explicit user action and must insert formatted source and citation metadata, not raw provider output.

decisions.title: Obsidian runtime law-provider HTTP uses requestUrl transport
decisions.notes: Law providers in Obsidian runtime must use Obsidian requestUrl transport, not global fetch, because browser/Electron transport behavior can fail for external law-provider websites even when Node validation succeeds.

decisions.title: Gesetze im Internet extraction prefers norm paragraph blocks
decisions.notes: Gesetze im Internet extraction must prefer actual norm paragraph blocks and exclude page chrome, navigation, footer, print links, and script/style/comment fragments from LawSection.text.
